content.text: is the conductor of an AutoGen group chat. It is itself a
— you start the conversation by calling

. On each turn, the manager reads the conversation history and decides which agent should speak next, then forwards control to that agent. The manager never generates task content itself — its only job is speaker selection and message routing.

attrs.alt: GroupChatManager decision loop: receive message, check transitions, select next speaker via selection method, dispatch to agent, increment round counter, loop until TERMINATE or max_round

content.text: . The LLM is used only when
— the manager uses it to decide who speaks next. For deterministic policies (round_robin, random, custom),

from autogen import GroupChat, GroupChatManager, AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent

llm_config = {"config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}]}

researcher = AssistantAgent("researcher", llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You research topics and summarise findings.")
coder = AssistantAgent("coder", llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You write Python code to solve tasks.")
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(
    "user_proxy",
    human_input_mode="NEVER",
    code_execution_config={"work_dir": "ws", "use_docker": False},
    is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""),
)

group_chat = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder],
    messages=[],
    max_round=10,
    speaker_selection_method="auto",
)

# The manager's llm_config is used only for speaker selection
manager = GroupChatManager(
    groupchat=group_chat,
    llm_config=llm_config,
)
user_proxy.initiate_chat(manager, message="Research merge sort and implement it.")

, the manager constructs a system prompt that lists all available agents and their descriptions, then sends the full conversation history to its LLM and asks it to name the next speaker. The response is matched against agent names case-insensitively.

# The auto-selection prompt includes each agent's name and system_message.
# You can override what the manager tells the LLM about each agent by
# setting a description that is different from the system_message:

researcher = AssistantAgent(
    "researcher",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You research topics and summarise findings concisely.",
    description="The researcher gathers and condenses factual information.",  # shown to manager
)
coder = AssistantAgent(
    "coder",
    llm_config=llm_config,
    system_message="You write Python code.",
    description="The coder writes and debugs Python code.",   # shown to manager
)

# The manager's LLM sees these descriptions when deciding who to pick.
# description is optional — if absent, system_message is used.
Inspect or override the exact prompt the manager sends to the LLM by accessing or setting
as placeholders that AutoGen fills in at runtime.

from autogen import GroupChat, GroupChatManager

# Override the selection prompt for clearer instructions
group_chat = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder],
    messages=[],
    max_round=10,
    select_speaker_message_template=(
        "You are moderating a conversation between these agents:\n{roles}\n\n"
        "Read the conversation carefully. Based on what has been said so far, "
        "which agent should speak next? Choose from: {agentlist}.\n"
        "Reply with just the agent name — no explanation."
    ),
)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=group_chat, llm_config=llm_config)
If the manager's LLM returns an agent name that doesn't match any agent (e.g., a hallucinated name), AutoGen raises a
to see the raw selection prompt and the model's response, which helps diagnose failures.

group_chat = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder],
    messages=[],
    max_round=10,
    select_speaker_auto_verbose=True,   # print the selection prompt + response
)

# If the LLM returns an invalid name, you'll see:
# > GroupChat select_speaker_auto prompt: ...
# > Selected speaker: goblin   <- not an agent name
# > ValueError: No agent named 'goblin' in the group chat.

# Mitigation: add retry logic using select_speaker_auto_llm_config
# with a higher temperature agent that handles ambiguity better:
group_chat_retry = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder],
    messages=[],
    max_round=10,
    max_retries_for_selecting_speaker=3,  # retry up to 3 times on invalid selection
)
For complete control over the next speaker, pass a Python callable to
. The function receives the last speaker agent and the
object and returns the next agent to speak.
LLM for speaker selection (set False for non-auto methods)
Additional termination check beyond TERMINATE keyword
Secondary safety cap on automated turns
Always NEVER — manager does not ask for human input
True suppresses all conversation output to stdout
to print every message including who the manager selected. Alternatively, examine the
list after the conversation — the manager's selection messages appear as role=

user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    manager,
    message="Research merge sort.",
    verbose=True,    # prints: [GroupChatManager] → researcher
)

# Or inspect post-run
manager_msgs = [
    m for m in group_chat.messages
    if m.get("name") == "GroupChatManager"
]
for m in manager_msgs:
    print(f"Manager selected: {m.get('content', '')}")

import pytest
from autogen import AssistantAgent, UserProxyAgent, GroupChat, GroupChatManager

def make_mock(name: str, reply: str) -> AssistantAgent:
    a = AssistantAgent(name, llm_config=False, human_input_mode="NEVER")
    a.register_reply(trigger=None,
                     reply_func=lambda r, msgs, s, cfg: (True, reply),
                     position=0)
    return a

def test_custom_router_picks_coder_on_code_keyword():
    researcher = make_mock("researcher", "Merge sort is O(n log n)")
    coder = make_mock("coder", "def merge_sort(arr): ... TERMINATE")
    proxy = UserProxyAgent("proxy", human_input_mode="NEVER",
                           code_execution_config=False,
                           is_termination_msg=lambda m: "TERMINATE" in (m.get("content") or ""))

    selected = []

    def track_router(last_speaker, gc):
        agents = {a.name: a for a in gc.agents}
        last = gc.messages[-1].get("content", "") if gc.messages else ""
        if "code" in last.lower():
            selected.append("coder")
            return agents["coder"]
        selected.append("researcher")
        return agents["researcher"]

    gc = GroupChat(agents=[proxy, researcher, coder], messages=[],
                   max_round=4, speaker_selection_method=track_router)
    mgr = GroupChatManager(groupchat=gc, llm_config=False)
    proxy.initiate_chat(mgr, message="Please code a solution.", max_turns=3)
    assert "coder" in selected
For advanced customisation such as logging every selection or integrating external orchestration logic, subclass

from autogen import GroupChatManager, GroupChat, ConversableAgent
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger("group_chat")

class LoggingGroupChatManager(GroupChatManager):
    def select_speaker(
        self, last_speaker: ConversableAgent, selector: "GroupChatManager"
    ) -> ConversableAgent:
        next_speaker = super().select_speaker(last_speaker, selector)
        logger.info(
            "Round %d: %s -> %s",
            len(self.groupchat.messages),
            last_speaker.name,
            next_speaker.name,
        )
        return next_speaker

# Use the custom manager in place of GroupChatManager
group_chat = GroupChat(agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder], messages=[], max_round=10)
manager = LoggingGroupChatManager(groupchat=group_chat, llm_config=llm_config)
Stall Detection in a Custom Selector
Detect and break loops where the same agent is selected repeatedly by tracking recent speakers in a custom selector function.

from autogen import ConversableAgent, GroupChat

def stall_aware_selector(last_speaker: ConversableAgent, gc: GroupChat):
    agents = gc.agents
    recent = [m.get("name") for m in gc.messages[-6:] if m.get("name")]
    last_3 = recent[-3:] if len(recent) >= 3 else recent
    if len(last_3) == 3 and all(n == last_speaker.name for n in last_3):
        # Rotate to next agent in the list
        idx = next((i for i, a in enumerate(agents) if a.name == last_speaker.name), 0)
        return agents[(idx + 1) % len(agents)]
    return None  # fall through to auto LLM selection

group_chat = GroupChat(
    agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder],
    messages=[],
    max_round=12,
    speaker_selection_method=stall_aware_selector,
)
Returning None from a custom selector:
configured on the GroupChat object. This lets you implement partial overrides: handle only the edge cases you care about and let the default handle the rest.
When LangSmith tracing is enabled, every LLM call the manager makes for speaker selection appears as a child run in the trace. Tag the manager's
to distinguish selection calls from task calls in the trace viewer.

import os
os.environ["LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2"] = "true"
os.environ["LANGCHAIN_API_KEY"] = "ls-..."
os.environ["LANGCHAIN_PROJECT"] = "group-chat-tracing"

# Tag the manager's LLM calls as speaker-selection to separate them in LangSmith
manager_llm_config = {
    "config_list": [{"model": "gpt-4o-mini", "api_key": "sk-..."}],
    "tags": ["speaker-selection"],
}

group_chat = GroupChat(agents=[user_proxy, researcher, coder], messages=[], max_round=10)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=group_chat, llm_config=manager_llm_config)
user_proxy.initiate_chat(manager, message="Research and implement bubble sort.")
GroupChatManager is a ConversableAgent whose sole role is selecting the next speaker and routing messages.
With speaker_selection_method='auto', it uses its own LLM to read the conversation and pick the best next agent.
Set select_speaker_message_template to customise the selection prompt and agent descriptions to guide selection.
Pass llm_config=False to GroupChatManager when using round_robin, random, or a custom selection function.
Custom speaker selection functions receive (last_speaker, groupchat) and return the next agent — zero LLM cost.
max_retries_for_selecting_speaker retries the LLM selection on invalid names; select_speaker_auto_verbose=True shows the raw prompt.
The manager's selection messages appear as role='assistant' with the manager's name in group_chat.messages.
